name: Cat's Eye Nebula
description: The Cat's Eye Nebula was a planetary nebula in the northern constellation of Draco, discovered by William Herschel on February 15, 1786.

name: NGC 6544
description: NGC 6544 is a small globular cluster visible in the constellation Sagittarius. It is magnitude 7.5, diameter 1 arcminute. It is less than 1 degree southeast of Messier 8, the Lagoon Nebula.

name: NGC 6553
description: NGC 6553 is a globular cluster in the constellation Sagittarius. Its Shapley–Sawyer Concentration Class is XI, meaning the star concentration is very loose even at the center; it has stars of magnitude 20 and dimmer.

name: NGC 6559
description: NGC 6559 is a star-forming region located at a distance of about 5000 light-years from Earth, in the constellation of Sagittarius, showing both emission (red) and reflection (bluish) regions. NGC 6559 is relatively small at only a few lightyears across.

name: NGC 6569
description: NGC 6569 is a globular cluster in the constellation Sagittarius. It is about 2 degrees south east of Gamma2 Sagittarii.

name: NGC 6572
description: NGC 6572 is a planetary nebula in the constellation Ophiuchus. It was discovered in 1825 by the German astronomer Friedrich Georg Wilhelm von Struve.

name: NGC 6584
description: NGC 6584 is a globular cluster in the constellation Telescopium that lies near Theta Arae and is 45000 light-years distant.

name: NGC 659
description: NGC 659, also known as the Yin-Yang Cluster is an open cluster in the Cassiopeia constellation. It was discovered by Caroline Herschel in 1783.

name: NGC 660
description: NGC 660 is a peculiar and unique polar-ring galaxy located approximately 45 million light-years from Earth in the Pisces constellation. It is the only such galaxy having, as its host, a "late-type lenticular galaxy".

name: NGC 6604
description: The cluster was discovered by William Herschel on July 15, 1784. It is located at a distance of 4,580 light years from the Sun, about 210 ly (65 pc) above the galactic plane.

name: NGC 6629
description: NGC 6629 is a planetary nebula in the constellation of Sagittarius, located above the "Teapot". The object formed when a star ejected its outer layers during the late stages of its evolution.

name: NGC 663
description: NGC 663 is a rich, bright open cluster in Cassiopeia about 6,850 light-years away, containing around 400 member stars including several Be emission-line stars.

name: NGC 6633
description: NGC 6633 is a large bright open cluster in the constellation Ophiuchus. Bright enough to be seen with the naked eye, the cluster is considered a fine object for binoculars or small telescopes.

name: NGC 6638
description: NGC 6638 is a globular cluster in the constellation Sagittarius. It is magnitude 9.5 and diameter 2 arc minutes, class VI. It is a half degree east of Lambda Sagittarii. It is a member of the Milky Way.

name: NGC 6642
description: NGC 6642 is a globular cluster located 26,700 light-years from Earth, in the constellation Sagittarius. Many "blue stragglers" have been spotted in this globular, and it is known to be lacking in low-mass stars.

name: NGC 6664
description: NGC 6664 is an open cluster in Scutum. It is located near NGC 6649.

name: NGC 6712
description: NGC 6712 is a globular cluster that was probably discovered by Le Gentil on July 9, 1749 when investigating the Milky Way star cloud in Aquila. He described it as a "true nebula," in contrast to the open star cluster M11.

name: NGC 6717
description: NGC 6717 is a globular cluster in the constellation Sagittarius, and is a member of the Palomar Globular Clusters group. Palomar 9 was discovered by William Herschel on August 7, 1784. It is located about 7,300 parsecs away from Earth.

name: NGC 6723
description: NGC 6723, also known as the Chandelier Cluster, is a globular cluster in the constellation Sagittarius. It is a class VII cluster with stars of magnitude 14 and dimmer.

name: Phantom Streak Nebula
description: NGC 6741, also known as the Phantom Streak Nebula, is located about 7000 light-years away in the constellation of Aquila.

name: NGC 6745
description: NGC 6745 is an irregular galaxy about 206 million light-years away in the constellation Lyra. It was discovered by French astronomer Édouard Stephan on 24 July 1879.

name: NGC 6752
description: NGC 6752 is the third brightest globular cluster in the sky, about 13,000 light-years away in Pavo, with stars precisely aged at around 11.78 billion years.

name: NGC 6760
description: NGC 6760 is a globular cluster in the constellation Aquila. It may have contributed to the formation of the open cluster Ruprecht 127 during NGC 6760's passage through the galactic disk 71 million years ago.

name: NGC 6781
description: It was discovered July 30, 1788 by the Anglo-German astronomer William Herschel. The nebula lies at a distance of 1,500 ly from the Sun.

name: NGC 6790
description: NGC 6790 is a young, compact planetary nebula with a high surface brightness located in the equatorial constellation of Aquila. Imaging by the Hubble Space Telescope shows elongated shells surrounding the central star.

name: Little Gem Nebula
description: The Little Gem Nebula or NGC 6818 is a planetary nebula located in the constellation of Sagittarius. It has magnitude 10 and oval diameter of 15 to 22 arcseconds with a 15th magnitude central star.

name: Barnard's Galaxy
description: Barnard's Galaxy is a Local Group irregular dwarf galaxy about 1.63 million light-years away in Sagittarius, containing numerous bright HII star-forming regions.

name: NGC 6823
description: NGC 6820 is a small emission nebula near the open cluster NGC 6823 in Vulpecula. The reflection nebula and cluster are embedded in a large faint emission nebula called Sh 2-86. The whole area of nebulosity is often referred to as NGC 6820.

name: NGC 6830
description: NGC 6830, also known as the Poodle Cluster, is an open cluster in Vulpecula. It contains about 150 members. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1784.

name: NGC 6834
description: NGC 6834 is a young open cluster of stars located about 10,850 light years from the Sun in the constellation Cygnus. It was discovered on July 17, 1784, by Anglo-German astronomer William Herschel.

name: NGC 6866
description: NGC 6866 is a young open cluster of stars in the northern constellation Cygnus. It was discovered by German astronomer Caroline Herschel on 23 July 1783.

name: Crescent Nebula
description: The Crescent Nebula is an emission nebula in the constellation Cygnus, about 5000 light-years away from Earth. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1792.

name: Blue Flash Nebula
description: NGC 6905, also known as the Blue Flash Nebula, is a planetary nebula in the constellation Delphinus. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1784.

name: NGC 6910
description: NGC 6910, also known as the Inchworn Cluster, is an open cluster in the constellation Cygnus. It was discovered by William Herschel on October 17, 1786.

name: NGC 6914
description: NGC 6914 is a reflection nebula located approximately 6,000 light-years away in the constellation of Cygnus, and was discovered by Édouard Stephan on August 29, 1881. Ultraviolet radiation from stars in the Cygnus OB2 association ionize the nebula's hydrogen.

name: NGC 6939
description: NGC 6939 is an open cluster in the constellation Cepheus. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1798. The cluster lies 2/3° northwest from the spiral galaxy NGC 6946. The cluster lies approximately 4,000 light years away and it is over a billion years old.

name: NGC 6940
description: NGC 6940 is an open cluster in the constellation Vulpecula. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1784. The cluster is nearly a billion years old and it is located 2,500 light years away. It is considered the finest open cluster in the constellation.

name: Western Veil Nebula
description: The Veil Nebula is a cloud of heated and ionized gas and dust in the constellation Cygnus.

name: Eastern Veil Nebula
description: The Veil Nebula is a cloud of heated and ionized gas and dust in the constellation Cygnus.

name: North America Nebula
description: The North America Nebula is an emission nebula in the constellation Cygnus, close to Deneb in the night sky. It is named because its shape resembles North America.

name: NGC 7008
description: NGC 7008, also known as the Fetus Nebula, is a planetary nebula with a diameter of approximately 1 light-year located at a distance of 2800 light years in northern Cygnus. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1787, in Slough, England.

name: Saturn Nebula
description: The Saturn Nebula is a planetary nebula in the constellation Aquarius. It appears as a greenish-yellowish hue in a small amateur telescope.

name: Iris Nebula
description: The Iris Nebula is a bright reflection nebula located about 1,300 light years from Earth in the constellation of Cepheus. It is six light-years across.

name: NGC 7026
description: NGC 7026, also known as the Cheeseburger Nebula, is a planetary nebula located 6000 light-years away, in the constellation of Cygnus. The central star of the planetary nebula has a spectral type of [WO3], indicating a spectrum similar to that of an oxygen-rich Wolf–Rayet star.

name: NGC 7027
description: NGC 7027, also known as the Jewel Bug Nebula or the Magic Carpet Nebula, is a very young and dense planetary nebula located around 3,000 light-years from Earth in the constellation Cygnus.

name: NGC 7044
description: NGC 7044 is an open cluster located in the Cygnus constellation. Containing approximately 742 stars, it was discovered by 18th century English astronomer William Herschel on 17 October 1786.

name: NGC 7129
description: NGC 7129, also known as the Small Cluster Nebula, or the Flower Bud Nebula, is a reflection nebula located 3,300 light years away in the constellation Cepheus. A young open cluster is responsible for illuminating the surrounding nebula.

name: NGC 7142
description: NGC 7142 is an open cluster about 6,200 light-years away in the constellation Cepheus.

name: NGC 7160
description: NGC 7160 is an open cluster in the constellation Cepheus. It was discovered by William Herschel on November 9, 1789.

name: NGC 7184
description: NGC 7184 is a barred spiral galaxy located in the constellation Aquarius. It is located at a distance of circa 100 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 7184 is about 236,000 light years across.

name: NGC 720
description: NGC 720 is an elliptical galaxy located in the constellation Cetus. It is located at a distance of about 80 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 720 is about 110,000 light years across.

name: NGC 7209
description: NGC 7209 is an open cluster in the constellation Lacerta. It was discovered by William Herschel on 19 October 1787.

name: NGC 7217
description: NGC 7217 is an unbarred spiral galaxy in the constellation Pegasus.

name: Helix Nebula
description: The Helix Nebula is a planetary nebula (PN) located in the constellation Aquarius. Discovered by Karl Ludwig Harding, most likely before 1824, this object is one of the closest of all the bright planetary nebulae to Earth.

name: NGC 7331
description: NGC 7331, also known as Caldwell 30, is an unbarred spiral galaxy about 13.427 megaparsecs away in the constellation Pegasus. It was discovered by William Herschel on 6 September 1784.

name: Wizard Nebula
description: NGC 7380 is a young open cluster of stars in the northern circumpolar constellation of Cepheus, discovered by Caroline Herschel in 1787. The surrounding emission nebulosity is known colloquially as the Wizard Nebula, which spans an angle of 25′.

name: NGC 7448
description: NGC 7448 is a spiral galaxy located in the constellation Pegasus. It is located at a distance of circa 80 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 7448 is about 60,000 light years across.

name: NGC 7510
description: NGC 7510 is an open cluster of stars located around 11,400 light years away in the constellation Cepheus, near the border with Cassiopeia. This cluster forms part of the Perseus Spiral Arm.

name: NGC 7538
description: NGC 7538, near the more famous Bubble Nebula, is located in the constellation Cepheus. It is located about 9,100 light-years from Earth.

name: NGC 7606
description: NGC 7606 is a spiral galaxy located in the constellation Aquarius. It is located at a distance of about 100 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 7606 is about 200,000 light years across.

name: NGC 7619
description: NGC 7619 is an elliptical galaxy located in the constellation Pegasus. NGC 7619 and NGC 7626 are the dominant and brightest members of the Pegasus galaxy cluster. Both of them were discovered by William Herschel on September 26, 1785.

name: Bubble Nebula
description: The Bubble Nebula, also known as NGC 7635, Sharpless 162, or Caldwell 11, is an H II region emission nebula in the constellation Cassiopeia. It lies close to the open cluster Messier 52.

name: NGC 7686
description: NGC 7686 is a moderately-sized open cluster in the constellation Andromeda, containing about 80 stars. At magnitude 5.6, it is an easy target for binoculars and small telescopes.

name: NGC 772
description: NGC 772 is a large unbarred spiral galaxy approximately 106 million light-years away in the constellation Aries. It was discovered by German-British astronomer William Herschel on 29 November 1785.

name: NGC 7723
description: NGC 7723 is a barred spiral galaxy located in the constellation Aquarius. It is located at a distance of about 90 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 7723 is about 95,000 light years across.

name: NGC 7727
description: NGC 7727 is a peculiar galaxy in the constellation Aquarius. It harbors two galactic nuclei, each containing a supermassive black hole, separated 1,600 light years apart. It was discovered by German-British astronomer William Herschel on 27 November 1785.

name: Caroline's Rose
description: NGC 7789 is an open cluster located around 7,600 light years from Earth in the constellation of Cassiopeia. It is intermediate-age cluster with estimates ranging from 1.4 to 1.7 billion years old. It has a mass of around 6,620.4±762.5 solar masses.

name: NGC 779
description: NGC 779 is a spiral galaxy seen edge-on, located in the constellation Cetus. It is located at a distance of circa 60 million light years from Earth, which, given its apparent dimensions, means that NGC 779 is about 70,000 light years across.

name: NGC 7793
description: NGC 7793 is a flocculent spiral galaxy in the southern constellation of Sculptor. It was discovered on July 14, 1826, by Scottish astronomer James Dunlop.

name: Cosmic Question Mark
description: NGC 7822, also known as the Question Mark Nebula, is a young star forming complex in the constellation of Cepheus. The complex encompasses the emission region designated Sharpless 171, and the young cluster of stars named Berkeley 59.

name: Double Cluster (h Per)
description: NGC 869 is an open cluster located around 7,460 light years away in the constellation of Perseus. The cluster is around 14 million years old. It is the west component of the Double Cluster with NGC 884.

name: Double Cluster (χ Per)
description: NGC 884 is an open cluster located 7640 light years away in the constellation of Perseus. It is the east component of the Double Cluster with NGC 869.

name: NGC 891
description: NGC 891 is an edge-on unbarred spiral galaxy about 30 million light-years away in the constellation Andromeda. It was discovered by William Herschel on October 6, 1784. The galaxy is a member of the NGC 1023 group of galaxies in the Local Supercluster. It has an H II nucleus.

name: NGC 908
description: NGC 908 is an unbarred spiral galaxy in the constellation Cetus. It was discovered on 20 September 1786 by William Herschel. This galaxy is 56 million light years away from Earth. It is the main galaxy in the NGC 908 group, which also includes NGC 899, NGC 907, and IC 223.

name: NGC 925
description: NGC 925 is a barred spiral galaxy located about 30 million light-years away in the constellation Triangulum. German-British astronomer William Herschel discovered this galaxy on 13 September 1784.

name: NGC 936
description: NGC 936 is a barred lenticular galaxy in the constellation Cetus. It is at a distance of about 60 million light-years away from Earth.

name: NGC 957
description: NGC 957 is a loosely bound open cluster located in the constellation Perseus. It has an apparent magnitude of 7.6 and an approximate size of 11 arc-minutes. It is young at less than 11 million years old.

name: Tulip Nebula
description: Sharpless 101 (Sh 2-101) is a H II region emission nebula located in the constellation Cygnus. It is sometimes also called the Tulip Nebula because it appears to resemble the outline of a tulip when imaged photographically.

name: Flying Bat Nebula
description: A faint emission nebula in Cepheus that surrounds the rare Squid Nebula (Ou4), a bipolar planetary-scale outflow only visible in OIII.

name: Cave Nebula
description: Sh 2-155 is a diffuse nebula in the constellation Cepheus, within a larger nebula complex containing emission, reflection, and dark nebulosity. It is widely known as the Cave Nebula, though that name was applied earlier to Ced 201, a different nebula in Cepheus.

name: Spaghetti Nebula
description: It was discovered in 1952 at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory by Grigory Shajn and his team using a Schmidt camera and a narrowband filter close to the Hydrogen Alpha transmission line. It is difficult to observe due to its extremely low surface brightness.
